title: Truth Delivered — Deliverable Closure Record
description: Schema-bound closure record for a completed deliverable. Captures what was actually delivered, with audit-traced links to merge commits, CI results, override records, and follow-up obligations. Per memory rule #19 (no-md-terminus), closure records must be schema-bound rather than prose-only. Per RULE-CI-DOCUMENTED-OVERRIDE-NOT-COMPLIANCE-1, this schema applies the forbidden-audit-paint-names constraint via allOf — the schema authors a closure RECORD, it must not itself permit audit-paint field names. A TRUTH-DELIVERED instance is authored after a deliverable reaches S7-merged and references the merged commit + actual outcomes. Instances live at schemas/_instances/truth-delivered/<deliverableId>.json.

$schema.description: Canonical schema URL. Must match exactly for instance to validate.

deliverableId.description: FK to deliverable.schema.json instance id. Same pattern: d-YYYY-MM-DD-slug.

deliveredAt.description: Timestamp of delivery (S7-merged transition). RFC 3339 / ISO 8601.

lifecycleState.description: Lifecycle state at time of TRUTH-DELIVERED authoring. Restricted to terminal states (a truth-delivered record is only meaningful after merge or revert).

closureStatus.description: closed-clean: all acceptance criteria met, no override, no carry-over debt. closed-with-debt: merged but with known issues captured in closureNotes.debt. reverted: deliverable was rolled back; closureNotes.revertReason required.

author.description: Who authored the deliverable. Matches deliverable.schema.json authority.author.

approver.description: Who approved (typically L4). Matches deliverable.schema.json authority.approver.

revertAuthority.description: Who has authority to revert. Typically same as approver.

authority.description: Authority block matching deliverable.schema.json shape for cross-schema consistency.

outputId.description: FK to deliverable.schema.json outputs[].id (e.g. OUT-1, OUT-2).

mergeCommit.description: Git SHA of merge commit. Short or full SHA accepted.

prUrl.description: Full URL to the merged PR.

mergeActor.description: GitHub username of the user who clicked merge. Distinct from authority.approver (governance) — this is operational.

ciResult.description: green: all required checks passed. red-overridden: CI was UNSTABLE/FAILING and merged via override label — ciFailure block required. skipped: no required checks ran.

ciFailure.description: Required when ciResult is red-overridden. Captures which checks failed and why override was applied.

failingChecks.description: Names of CI checks with conclusion FAILURE.

overrideRationale.description: Why override was applied. Cites substrate evidence that failure was not introduced by this PR.

overrideLabel.description: GitHub label that authorized the override (e.g. override-unstable-merge).

outputs.description: Actual delivered outputs with audit-traced links to PRs, commits, and CI results.

outcome.description: Plain-language summary of what was delivered.

debt.description: Known issues, deferred work, or technical debt accepted as part of merging. Required if closureStatus = closed-with-debt.

overrideRecord.description: If any rule was overridden during the deliverable (e.g. L4 conscious CI override per memory #15), describe the override and its substrate basis here.

spikeOutcome.description: If a SPIKE was entered during the deliverable (per memory #17), capture which terminal (COLLAPSE-AND-RESUME, DELETE-AND-FU, DELETE-AND-NEW-DELIVERABLE) was reached.

revertReason.description: Required if lifecycleState = reverted. Why the merge was rolled back.

closureNotes.description: Free-form prose context that does not fit into structured fields. Per memory #19 — prose-inside-instance is the legitimate home for prose.

relatedFollowUps.description: FU IDs closed by this delivery, or newly filed as carryover. Cross-repo FU IDs are valid (e.g. EB-side FUs that SR delivery unblocks).

evidenceClass.description: What evidence this delivery provides for the rule. Matches rule.schema.json transitions[].evidenceClass field.

transitionTriggered.description: If this delivery triggered a rule lifecycleStatus or tier transition, capture it.

rulePipelineEvidence.description: Cross-chat relay payload: when a deliverable provides evidence that promotes a rule's maturity tier, this array carries the linkage.

actor.description: Who initiated the transition (chat agent, terminal agent, Erik L4).

event.description: What transition occurred (e.g. authored, instance-validation-run, merged, closed).

transitions.description: Audit trail of TRUTH-DELIVERED record itself (when it was authored, by whom, what events updated it).
